mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals an accommodative monetary regime that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days when the policy rate remains at the effective lower bound.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 45% of observations where FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months) signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending power and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the correlation between 3-month UNRATE change and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below the 12-month trend while housing starts (HOUST) simultaneously contract, indicating weakening aggregate demand. This joint deterioration in consumption and investment signals reduced corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth < 2% AND HOUST < 1400k units
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances where both conditions are met, or Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) expands at an accelerating rate (month-over-month growth > 0.3%), signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains that support corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 48% of triggered instances, or the mean forward return in high-growth INDPRO months is not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100k new jobs per month while the unemployment rate remains elevated (UNRATE > 5.5%), indicating a stalled labor market recovery. This interaction signals weakening wage pressure and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 52% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3% year-over-year while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 2%, indicating monetary policy is accommodative relative to inflation. This real rate compression signals future tightening expectations and equity risk premium expansion, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary policy lag regime with rising inflation
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the real rate spread does not exhibit significant predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling an economy approaching stall-speed conditions. This always-on trend mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations compress and earnings forecasts are revised downward.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations where GDPC1 growth is below 1.5% annualized, or the correlation between GDP growth and subsequent 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) surge above 1700k units annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously accelerate, indicating robust consumer demand and residential investment. This joint demand expansion signals strong earnings growth and economic momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 48% of triggered instances, or the interaction term exhibits Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.
